title: Provenance
description: Verifiable provenance for a record or edge. Every public data point must be traceable to a source, a retrieval time, a license, and a content hash so any claim can be replayed and audited.

source_url.description: Public URL or API query the record/edge was derived from. Use 'synthetic://<name>' for original synthetic data authored for this project.

retrieved_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of retrieval or generation.

license.description: SPDX identifier or short license/terms note for the source (e.g. 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', 'CC0-1.0', 'public-metadata').

content_hash.description: sha256:<hex> hash of the canonical content this record/edge was derived from. 'TODO' is permitted only in templates, never in committed dataset records.

transform.description: Reproducible command or pipeline step that produced this record/edge from the source.

method.description: Extraction channel for a relation edge (see edge.schema.json). Optional on records.

observed.description: True when directly observed from a source; false when inferred. Inferred edges should carry lower confidence.
